textPlain: Seems reasonable. I have a Duemilanove and an Uno R2 that I haven't used in ages but Arduino stuff has always been open as far as I remember. I really can't bring myself to pull out the pitchforks here. They've earned the trust from me. It's been over a decade now.
I don't get it, do we keep the pitchforks out, or do we stash the pitchforks?
Sorry, you got bought by Qualcomm and that was suicide.
They say "privacy" a lot on that blog page, but that very page runs surveillance capitalism trackers from Facebook, Twitter, Google, and others.
This allays my concerns. I appreciate the response to this community’s concerns.
"We are Arduino. We are open. We’re not going anywhere."-- statement from Qualcomm without a single human being's name on it
If the only restriction on reverse engineering is their cloud SaaS, why was everyone up in arms?Or is this Arduino trying to save face?
